name: Spicy Szechwan Chicken
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound Boneless chicken, cut into bite-size pieces
Cornstarch solution (1 part cornstarch, 2 parts water)
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch broccoli in boiling water with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 tablespoon oil for 2-3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
2. Heat wok with 2 tablespoons oil over high heat. Add garlic and ginger; stir-fry for 10-15 seconds.
3. Add chicken to the wok; stir-fry until cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
4. Stir in white wine, soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, hot bean paste, and soup stock. Cook until the sauce thickens slightly.
5. Return broccoli to the wok with the cornstarch solution. Mix well and serve.
Notes: I used my own hot sauce, which worked well. It is tempting to cook the chicken longer than 3 minutes, but don't! It will be cooked by the time you thicken the sauce, and it will be very tender. If you cook it until ready and then make the sauce, it may be slightly overcooked. I find it easier to have the sauce ingredients premixed, as I could never get them into the wok fast enough.
